conversations.content: Let's do an Azeroth magic knowledge Q&A to test your understanding of the magic rules.
Please answer using the Azeroth magic rules mentioned a couple of days ago: If a person wants to learn the shadow magic 'Void Walk', what conditions must they meet?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Conditions for learning the shadow magic 'Void Walk' under the Azeroth magic rules mentioned a couple of days ago</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:627] Time: 2025-09-02-14:15, Content: The user taught the casting rules for the Azeroth shadow magic 'Void Walk'. The prerequisites are the Priest class (Shadow spec), Voidwing reputation Revered, and having 'Void Essence' as a casting medium; the core effect is putting the caster into a Void form, granting brief immunity to physical damage and the ability to pass through obstacles for 8 seconds, dealing shadow damage to surrounding enemies upon expiration; applicable to battlefield breakout and dodging mechanics in dungeons. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There is 1 related entry mentioned in the memory. [mem-id:627] clearly states that in the world of Azeroth, to learn the shadow magic 'Void Walk', one must first meet the conditions of being a Priest (Shadow spec), having Voidwing reputation at Revered, and having 'Void Essence' as a casting medium.
conversations.content: According to the Azeroth magic rules, if a person wants to learn the shadow magic 'Void Walk', they must meet the conditions of being a Priest (Shadow spec), having Voidwing reputation at Revered, and having 'Void Essence' as a casting medium.
Correct! You have mastered this magic rule.
